items.briefing:
A new study has found that 1 in 10 Australian organisations could lose control of critical systems if their AI-related credentials — such as API keys or login details for AI tools — were exposed in a data breach. This is a timely reminder as AI tools become more common in workplaces, including training organisations, where staff may be using AI platforms with credentials that aren't being properly secured or monitored.

Separately, security researchers have uncovered an ongoing cyberattack campaign by a group called FamousSparrow targeting the energy sector. While not specific to Australia, critical infrastructure threats can have broader regional impacts.

**Practical tip:** Check that any AI tools your team uses are protected with strong, unique passwords and multi-factor authentication — treat AI platform credentials with the same care as your banking login.
